mapping.a_element: small gray-matter regions of the brain as network nodes
mapping.b_element: employees as nodes of the Tacit Knowledge Transfer Graph

mapping.a_element: structural connectivity strength between brain regions
mapping.b_element: edge weights encoding knowledge-transfer links between employees

brief_justification: Paper A is an empirical/statistical analysis of neuroimaging data that measures which brain regions show increased or decreased static structural connectivity in dementia, with no governing dynamical law; Paper B is a heat-diffusion dynamical model on a graph used to simulate knowledge spread and optimize scheduling. Their only overlap is the generic 'represent the system as a graph' framing — Paper A never runs a diffusion process — so the transferable machinery (the heat-diffusion equation driving dynamics) is present in B but absent in A.
